intent: Real AVC capture: logrotate_t denied read on shadow_t:file. The canonical anchor scenario from f4. Captured via semodule -DB on Rocky 8 kernel 4.18.

denials.raw_avc: type=AVC msg=audit(1780454002.048:9146): avc:  denied  { read } for  pid=8742 comm="mycat" name="f1" dev="vda5" ino=109053166 scontext=system_u:system_r:logrotate_t:s0 tcontext=unconfined_u:object_r:shadow_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: omits auth_read_shadow macro: drops typeattribute can_read_shadow_passwords, drops open/getattr/ioctl/lock padding, drops etc_t:dir grant
oracle.audit2why_note: Must run audit2why with semodule -DB active AND -p policy.31. Without -DB, audit2why says Unknown/dontaudit.

kernel_4_18_field_set_notes: AVC record fields present: avc-verdict, perms, pid, comm, name, dev, ino, scontext, tcontext, tclass, permissive
SYSCALL companion record: arch, syscall=257 (openat), success=no, exit=-13 (EACCES), a0-a3, items, ppid, pid, auid, uid-gid-euid etc., tty, ses, comm, exe, subj, key
ENRICHED decoded fields in SYSCALL (kernel 4.18): ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=openat AUID=unset UID=root ... appended immediately after key=(null) with NO SPACE delimiter
PATH companion record: item=0, name, inode, dev, mode, ouid, ogid, rdev, obj (SELinux context), nametype=NORMAL, cap_fp, cap_fi, cap_fe, cap_fver, cap_frootid
ENRICHED in PATH: OUID=root OGID=root appended at end without delimiter
policy.31 vs policy.33: Rocky 8 compiles policy at version 31 (checkmodule module language 4-19), but the kernel-supported policyvers is 33. This is a compiled-vs-runtime version difference.

cross_version_field_set_comparison.rocky8_kernel_4_18: policy.31, AVC fields as above, ENRICHED with no-space suffix
cross_version_field_set_comparison.rocky9_kernel_5_14: policy.33 (expected), check rocky9 anchor for comparison
cross_version_field_set_comparison.rocky10_kernel_6_12: policy.33 (expected), check rocky10 anchor for comparison
cross_version_field_set_comparison.finding: Field SET is identical across kernel versions (same AVC format since kernel 3.x). The ENRICHED decoding appended format differs: kernel 4.18 appends with no delimiter vs newer kernels which use a space before the decoded field.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: Naive impl may include too-broad allow from auth_read_shadow macro. The expected narrow allow is exactly: allow logrotate_t shadow_t:file { read }; -- no open, no getattr, no etc_t.

note_dontaudit: WITHOUT semodule -DB, this AVC is dontaudit'd in the stock policy and never appears in audit.log. semodule -DB is required to capture it. semodule -B restores the original policy. This is the canonical example from f4 section 0 (the blocker discovered during grounding).
